title: GARCH Volatility Estimation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: GARCH Volatility Estimation: GARCH(1,1) model: estimate conditional volatility and model parameters from return series via maximum likelihood. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.garch.conditional_variance…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; structural_break_in_vol_regime, fat_tail_misspecification set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: r_t = sigma_t * z_t, sigma_t ** 2 = omega + alpha*r_{t-1}^2 + beta*sigma_{t-1}^2
E.world_state_x: conditional variance sigma_t ** 2 series and GARCH parameters (omega, alpha, beta)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered conditional_vol_series is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); structural_break_in_vol_regime dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian student t sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: QLIKE < 0.1 for well-specified GARCH model. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |structural_break_in_vol_regime|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(T) for log-likelihood recursion per evaluation per iteration
